messages.content: Please proofread the following English: Total RNA was isolated from mesenteric lymph node B-cells of WT or uc.106-/- mice aged 8-10 weeks using RNAiso Plus (Takara, Japan, 9109). The RNA samples were sequenced by Anoroad Gene Technology (Beijing) Co., LTD. PolyA RNA was subsequently purified from 50-100 ng total RNA by magnetic beads with Oligo (dT). After  library preparation, sequencing was performed using the Illumina platform, following a PE150 sequencing strategy.The sequencing strategy was the PE150. Each group was sequenced in triplet sample.

Total RNA was isolated from mesenteric lymph node B-cells of WT or uc.106-/- mice aged 8-10 weeks using RNAiso Plus (Takara, Japan, 9109). The RNA samples were sequenced by Anoroad Gene Technology (Beijing) Co., LTD. PolyA RNA was subsequently purified from 50-100 ng total RNA by magnetic beads with Oligo(dT). After library preparation, sequencing was performed using the Illumina platform, following a PE150 sequencing strategy. The sequencing strategy was PE150 with each group sequenced in triplet samples.

Please proofread the following English:  uc.106 RNAs were synthesized by in vitroin vitro using RiboTM T7 RNA production system (RIBOBIO, China, C11001) following the manufacturer's instructions. The 3’-end desthiobiotin-labeled uc.106 RNA probes used in the RNA pulldown were generated with an RNA 3’ End Desthiobiotinylation Kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA, 20163) according to the manufacturer’s instructions. The RNA pulldown was conducted using a Magnetic RNA-Protein Pull-Down Kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA, 20164). In brief, the desthiobiotin-labeled uc.106 RNA probes were captured by streptavidin magnetic beads and then mixed with cell extracts from 38B9 or Romas cells in Cell Lysis Buffer (Keygen, China, KGP701) in a protein-RNA-binding buffer. Then incubated for 30-60 minutes at 4℃ with agitation or rotation. After the incubation, proteins bound to uc.106 RNA probes were eluted and subsequently separated via sodium dodecyl sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE). The gel was stained using a silver staining method. Then, specific proteins that interacted with uc.106 were identified through mass spectrometry (MS) analysis. The binding proteins of uc.106 and uc.106-Antisense in this study are listed in Additional file 1: Table S4.

uc.106 RNAs were synthesized in vitro using the RiboTM T7 RNA production system (RIBOBIO, China, C11001) following the manufacturer's instructions. The 3’-end desthiobiotin-labeled uc.106 RNA probes used in the RNA pulldown were generated with an RNA 3’ End Desthiobiotinylation Kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA, 20163) according to the manufacturer's instructions. The RNA pulldown was conducted using a Magnetic RNA-Protein Pull-Down Kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA, 20164). In brief, the desthiobiotin-labeled uc.106 RNA probes were captured by streptavidin magnetic beads and then mixed with cell extracts from 38B9 or Romas cells in Cell Lysis Buffer (Keygen, China, KGP701) in a protein-RNA-binding buffer. The mixture was then incubated for 30-60 minutes at 4℃ with agitation or rotation. After the incubation, proteins bound to uc.106 RNA probes were eluted and subsequently separated via sodium dodecyl sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE). The gel was stained using a silver staining method. Then, specific proteins that interacted with uc.106 were identified through mass spectrometry (MS) analysis. The binding proteins of uc.106 and uc.106-Antisense in this study are listed in Additional file 1: Table S4.

Please proofread the following English: The RIP assay was performed using RNA Immunoprecipitation Kit (Geneseed, China, P0101) following the manufacturer’s instructions. Briefly, Approximately 1 × 107 38B9 or Romas cells were seeded and lysed with lysate buffer for 10 minutes on ice. Anti-METTL3 antibody (Abcam, USA, ab195352) and anti-rabbit IgG (Beyotime, China, A0216) were incubated with 50μL magnetic beads before being added to the cell lysates. Following incubation and binding, the magnetic beads were washed extensively. A filtration column was used to remove DNA, and purify RNA from the samples. The purified RNAs were analyzed by RT-qPCR, while proteins were extracted using pre-cold acetone and analyzed via immunoblotting. The relative enrichment was normalized to IgG using the formula: 2-(2Ct [IP] - Ct [IgG]).

The RIP assay was performed using the RNA Immunoprecipitation Kit (Geneseed, China, P0101) following the manufacturer's instructions. Briefly, approximately 1 × 107 38B9 or Romas cells were seeded and lysed with lysate buffer for 10 minutes on ice. Anti-METTL3 antibody (Abcam, USA, ab195352) and anti-rabbit IgG (Beyotime, China, A0216) were incubated with 50μL magnetic beads before being added to the cell lysates. Following incubation and binding, the magnetic beads were washed extensively. A filtration column was used to remove DNA and purify RNA from the samples. The purified RNAs were analyzed by RT-qPCR, while proteins were extracted using pre-cold acetone and analyzed via immunoblotting. The relative enrichment was normalized to IgG using the formula: 2-(2Ct [IP] - Ct [IgG]).

Please proofread the following English: The crystallography structure of methyltransferase like 3 (METTL3) (PDB ID: 6Y4G[1]) was obtained from the Research Collaboratory for Structural Bioinformatics (RCSB) Protein Data Bank. The secondary structure of uc.106 was predicted using the RNAfold software[3]. The secondary structure of the uc.106 was predicted using the software[3] and the 3D model of uc.106 was computationally constructed using 3dRNA software[4]. Then, molecular docking calculations were performed utilizing AutoDock Vina software. Molecular docking calculations were performed utilizing the AutoDock Vina software. For these calculations, pdbqt files were prepared for both the proteins and ligands following the AutoDock protocol. All docking parameters were conserved to their default values, except for the maximum number of energy evaluations (eval) and the number of genetic algorithms (GA) runs. The docking grid was established at the binding site for the receptor with a grid size of 40 Å* 40 Å *40 Å, and the grid spacing was set to 0.375 Å. Gasteiger atomic partial charges were assigned to all investigated ligands. PyMol (PyMOL Molecular Graphics System, version 1.7) program (https://pymol.org) was applied for visualizing to obtain hydrogen bond, hydrophobic, and electrostatic interactions[5].

The crystallography structure of methyltransferase like 3 (METTL3) (PDB ID: 6Y4G[1]) was obtained from the Research Collaboratory for Structural Bioinformatics (RCSB) Protein Data Bank. The secondary structure of uc.106 was predicted using the RNAfold software[3]. The 3D model of uc.106 was computationally constructed using 3dRNA software[4]. Then, molecular docking calculations were performed using AutoDock Vina software. For these calculations, pdbqt files were prepared for both the proteins and ligands following the AutoDock protocol. All docking parameters were kept at their default values, except for the maximum number of energy evaluations (eval) and the number of genetic algorithms (GA) runs. The docking grid was established at the binding site for the receptor with a grid size of 40 Å * 40 Å * 40 Å, and the grid spacing was set to 0.375 Å. Gasteiger atomic partial charges were assigned to all investigated ligands. The PyMol (PyMOL Molecular Graphics System, version 1.7) program (https://pymol.org) was used for visualizing hydrogen bonds, hydrophobic interactions, and electrostatic interactions[5].

Please proofread the following English: The Binding energies of ligands with the protein were computed using molecular mechanics-generalized born surface area (MM-GBSA) approach with a GB model[6]. The binding energies (DGbinding) were calculated for the molecular docking complexes using the formula:
DGbinding = GComplex - (Gligand + Greceptor)
where the energy term (G) is estimated as:
G = Evdw + Eele + GGB + GSA
With Evdw, Eele, GGB, and GSA as the van der Waals, electrostatic, general Born solvation and surface area energies, respectively. For the inhibitors, entropy contributions were neglected.

The binding energies of the ligands with the protein were computed using the molecular mechanics-generalized born surface area (MM-GBSA) approach with a GB model[6]. The binding energies (ΔGbinding) were calculated for the molecular docking complexes using the formula:
ΔGbinding = GComplex - (Gligand + Greceptor)
where the energy term (G) is estimated as:
G = Evdw + Eele + GGB + GSA
Here, Evdw, Eele, GGB, and GSA represent the van der Waals, electrostatic, general Born solvation, and surface area energies, respectively. In the case of inhibitors, entropy contributions were neglected.

Please proofread the following English: Cytoplasmic and nuclear proteins were extracted and purified using the Nuclear Protein and Cytoplasmic Protein Extraction Kit (Beyotime, China, P0028) following the manufacturer’s instructions. Brifely, cells were washed with PBS and collected by centrifugation. Two million cells were resuspended in 200 μL of Cytoplasmic protein extraction reagent A containing 1% PMSF. The suspension was vortexed for 5 seconds at the highest speed and incubated on ice for 15 minutes. Subsequently, 10 μL of Cytoplasmic Protein Extraction Reagent B was added, followed by another 5-second vortex and 1-minute incubation on ice. The mixture was then centrifuged at 4℃ at 12,000 rpm for 5 minutes. The supernatant was the extracted cytoplasmic protein. For nuclear protein extraction, 50μL of Nuclear Protein Extraction Reagent supplemented with PMSF was added to the pellet, followed by a 30-second vortex and incubation on ice for 30 minutes. This mixture was also centrifuged at 4℃ at 12,000 rpm for 5 minutes, yielding the extracted nuclear proteins.

Cytoplasmic and nuclear proteins were extracted and purified using the Nuclear Protein and Cytoplasmic Protein Extraction Kit (Beyotime, China, P0028) following the manufacturer's instructions. Briefly, cells were washed with PBS and collected by centrifugation. Two million cells were resuspended in 200 μL of Cytoplasmic protein extraction reagent A containing 1% PMSF. The suspension was vortexed for 5 seconds at the highest speed and incubated on ice for 15 minutes. Subsequently, 10 μL of Cytoplasmic Protein Extraction Reagent B was added, followed by another 5-second vortex and 1-minute incubation on ice. The mixture was then centrifuged at 4°C at 12,000 rpm for 5 minutes. The supernatant contained the extracted cytoplasmic proteins. For nuclear protein extraction, 50 μL of Nuclear Protein Extraction Reagent supplemented with PMSF was added to the pellet, followed by a 30-second vortex and incubation on ice for 30 minutes. This mixture was also centrifuged at 4°C at 12,000 rpm for 5 minutes, resulting in the extracted nuclear proteins.

Please proofread the following English: The EpiQuik™ m6A RNA Methylation Quantification Kit (EpiGentek, USA, P-9005) was utilized to quantify the total m6A level in B-cells isolated from mouse mesenteric lymph nodes. Briefly, 200ng of total RNA was combined with specific capture and detection antibodies in each well which was used for subsequent detection. Signals from m6A were quantified at 450nm wavelength, and the m6A content of RNA was calculated in accordance with the manufacturer’s protocol.

The EpiQuik™ m6A RNA Methylation Quantification Kit (EpiGentek, USA, P-9005) was used to quantify the total m6A level in B-cells isolated from mouse mesenteric lymph nodes. Briefly, 200 ng of total RNA was combined with specific capture and detection antibodies in each well for subsequent detection. Signals from m6A were quantified at a wavelength of 450nm, and the m6A content of RNA was calculated according to the manufacturer's protocol.

Please proofread the following English: The m6A dot blot assay was employed to quantify the total m6A level in B-cells from the mouse mesenteric lymph nodes. Briefly, 1000ng of total RNA samples were spotted onto a nitrocellulose membrane (Merk Memillipore, USA, HATF00010) and crosslinked by UV irradiation. The membrane was stained with 0.02% methylene blue (Solarbio, China, G1301) in 0.3M sodium acetate. Subsequently, the membrane was washed with 1x PBST buffer, blocked with 5% non-fat milk in TBST, and incubated overnight at 4 °C with an anti-m6A antibody (proteintech, 68055-1-Ig). Afterward, the membrane was probed with an Anti-rabbit IgG HRP-conjugated antibody (Cell signaling technology, USA, 7074s). The chemiluminescent signal was visualized using the SuperPico ECL Chemiluminescence Kit (Vazyme, E422). Protein intensity was quantified using ImageJ. The antibodies used in this study are listed in Additional file 1: Table S3.

The m6A dot blot assay was used to quantify the total m6A level in B-cells from the mouse mesenteric lymph nodes. Briefly, 1000 ng of total RNA samples were spotted onto a nitrocellulose membrane (Merk Memillipore, USA, HATF00010) and crosslinked by UV irradiation. The membrane was stained with 0.02% methylene blue (Solarbio, China, G1301) in 0.3 M sodium acetate. Subsequently, the membrane was washed with 1x PBST buffer, blocked with 5% non-fat milk in TBST, and incubated overnight at 4°C with an anti-m6A antibody (Proteintech, 68055-1-Ig). Afterward, the membrane was probed with an anti-rabbit IgG HRP-conjugated antibody (Cell Signaling Technology, USA, 7074s). The chemiluminescent signal was visualized using the SuperPico ECL Chemiluminescence Kit (Vazyme, E422). Protein intensity was quantified using ImageJ. The antibodies used in this study are listed in Additional file 1: Table S3.

Please proofread the following English: The MeRIP-Seq procedure was conducted by Ribo Bio (Guangzhou, China) Co., LTD. Briefly, total RNA was extracted from mesenteric lymph node B-cells of both WT and uc.106-/- mice aged 8-10 weeks using RNAiso Plus (Takara, Japan, 9109). RNA fragmentation was achieved through sonication. Following m6A-IP and library preparation, sequencing was performed on the Illumina platform according to the manufacturer’s instructions. The sequencing strategy employed was PE150, and each group consisted of 50 mice.

The MeRIP-Seq procedure was conducted by Ribo Bio (Guangzhou, China) Co., LTD. Briefly, total RNA was extracted from mesenteric lymph node B-cells of both WT and uc.106-/- mice aged 8-10 weeks using RNAiso Plus (Takara, Japan, 9109). RNA fragmentation was achieved through sonication. Following m6A-IP and library preparation, sequencing was performed on the Illumina platform according to the manufacturer's instructions. The sequencing strategy employed was PE150, and each group consisted of 50 mice.

Please proofread the following English: 1×106 B-cells from WT or uc.106-/- mice mesenteric lymph nodes were seeded into 6-well plates. Then cells were treated with actinomycin D (10μg/mL, MedChemExpress, USA, HY-17559) for 0, 10, 20, and 40 minutes. Total RNA was isolated using TRIzol and quantified by qRT-PCR. The expression of mRNA at the indicated time points was calculated and normalized to GAPDH.

1×106 B-cells from WT or uc.106-/- mice mesenteric lymph nodes were seeded into 6-well plates. Then, the cells were treated with actinomycin D (10μg/mL, MedChemExpress, USA, HY-17559) for 0, 10, 20, and 40 minutes. Total RNA was isolated using TRIzol and quantified by qRT-PCR. The expression of mRNA at the indicated time points was calculated and normalized to GAPDH.
